name: Robert e. Lee Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Spray three 8-inch round cake pans with vegetable cooking spray.
3. In a large bowl, beat egg whites until they hold stiff but moist peaks.
4. In another large bowl, beat egg yolks until lemon-colored and very light.
5. Slowly beat in 1 1/2 cups sugar.
6. Gently fold in the egg whites into the yolk mixture.
7. Gradually add flour, salt, and 1 tablespoon lemon juice, mixing only enough to blend the flour.
8. Divide the batter equally among the prepared pans.
9. Bake for 35-45 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted into the center comes out clean.
10. In a medium bowl, combine citrus peels, juices, 1 1/2 cups sugar, and 1 cup shredded coconut.
11. Remove cake layers from pans to trays.
12. Spoon the citrus mixture over the tops of warm layers; let cool.
13. Stack the layers on a serving plate.
14. Let stand several hours before serving to allow the cake to absorb liquid.
15. To serve, sprinkle with remaining 1/2 cup shredded coconut.
Makes: 1 (8-inch) layer cake
